query: Letters of the Living who met Bahá'u'lláh
intent: Subset of the 18 Letters with a cited meeting; no unsupported members

query: who accompanied the Báb on His pilgrimage to Mecca
intent: Quddús accompanied the Báb to Mecca/Ḥijáz

query: companions imprisoned with Bahá'u'lláh in the Síyáh-Chál
intent: Fellow prisoners of the Black Pit, 1852

query: who met the Báb in Shíráz
intent: Mullá Ḥusayn, the first to believe

query: who was martyred at Fort Ṭabarsí
intent: Ṭabarsí martyrs — Mullá Ḥusayn, Quddús, others

query: Bábís who died at Zanján
intent: Ḥujjat and the Zanján upheaval martyrs

query: the martyrs of Nayríz
intent: Vaḥíd and the 1850 Nayríz cohort; not 1853/1909 namesakes

query: who was beheaded
intent: Persons whose cited death cause is beheading

query: the seven martyrs of Tihran
intent: The Seven Martyrs group roster (deterministic)

query: the Báb's amanuensis
intent: Siyyid Ḥusayn-i-Yazdí (distinct from Bahá'u'lláh's amanuensis)

query: women in the Dawn-Breakers
intent: Ṭáhirih and other cited female figures

query: clerics who embraced the Bábí Faith
intent: Mullás/Siyyids who converted

query: who first recognized the Báb
intent: Mullá Ḥusayn — the first Letter of the Living

query: disciples martyred alongside Quddús at Bárfurúsh
intent: Ṭabarsí survivors killed at Bárfurúsh with Quddús

query: who conferred the title Bahá'u'lláh
intent: The Báb conferred the title

query: the mother and wife of the Báb
intent: Khadíjih-Bagum (wife) and His mother

query: who was the Báb's uncle
intent: Ḥájí Mírzá Siyyid ‘Alí, Khál-i-A‘ẓam

query: governors who persecuted the Bábís
intent: Opponent-side officials with cited persecution acts

query: who declared his mission at Badasht
intent: The Badasht three — Ṭáhirih, Bahá'u'lláh, Quddús

query: believers who attained the presence of Bahá'u'lláh in Baghdád
intent: Baghdád-period companions, period-correct
